weather_line: Clear above the ridgeline · atmospheric conditions disputed · bring a jacket

stories.headline: 'Disputed (Atmospheric)' Turns Out to Mean Exactly That, and Also Something More: The Third Fold Resolved at First Hand
stories.byline: By our Highland Correspondent, filing from elevation
body: They reached the ridgeline at half past nine. The brass duck's coordinates were precise to within four feet — which is to say, precisely the four feet at the centre of the dispute. The Jeep was parked on a fog-shelf below. Christine was already walking the edge before Steve had the handbrake fully set.

The 'atmospheric' notation in the 1962 survey records referred, it turns out, to a seasonal inversion layer that pools in the hollow between the two ridge arms approximately ninety mornings a year, creating a pocket of fog that sits at exactly the elevation where both survey teams conducted their measurements — on different mornings, in different conditions, reading a landscape that looked, in fog, like two slightly different landscapes. Both teams were correct. The ridge has two elevations. The map has one line. The Third Fold, it transpires, was not an opinion about a contour. It was the map acknowledging it could not hold both things at once.

Christine confirmed which survey team had been closer — the eastern one, as the pencil mark in the margin had quietly argued since Thursday — and said so aloud for the first time. Steve nodded in the way of a man who rated himself at nine out of ten and has just learned the final point was never in doubt. Then the fog lifted, briefly, and the view was extraordinary, and neither of them said anything about surveys for quite a while after that.

stories.headline: Orvell Was Already There: How She Got to the Ridgeline Before Anyone and Why She Left a Second Brass Duck That Was Actually the First One All Along
body: Margaret Orvell, 63, was seated on a flat rock at the disputed ridgeline's eastern arm when Christine and Steve arrived. She had a flask, a folding chair, and the unhurried expression of a woman who had been factoring in the fog inversion since the 1990s. She said 'Good morning' and did not explain how she had arrived before them, and both Christine and Steve had the sense that asking would have been missing the point entirely.

Orvell produced a second brass duck from her jacket pocket and set it on the rock between them. This one wore a small tartan sash. On the base, where the first duck had carried coordinates, this one had a single line: 'The eastern team. I checked in 2003.' Christine looked at it for a moment. Then she laughed, which Orvell treated as the correct answer.

The three of them shared the flask — it contained tea, which tasted of somewhere specific that none of them tried to name — and watched the inversion layer shift in the hollow below. Before she left, Orvell said the trail was theirs now if they wanted to run it. Steve was already looking at the northern descent. The brass duck has been placed beside the first one on the shelf. They now form a pair, which feels correct.

stories.headline: Galactic Liaison Office Sends Seasonal Greetings to the American Wizarding Territories; Memo Describes 'Celebratory Ordinance' as Technically Authorised
body: The Ministry's Office of Extragalactic Relations issued a brief statement this morning acknowledging the American Wizarding Territories' annual Independence Observance, describing the traditional firework displays as 'technically within the permitted-ordinance framework, pending review of last year's incident involving a barge and a dragon reserve.' The statement was three paragraphs long, which is two paragraphs more than last year, and is being read as a warm gesture.

A postcard arrived at the Prophet offices from the galactic liaison station — not from Andrew, whose most recent dispatch is expected separately, but from the station's duty officer, who had attached a small illustration of what appeared to be a lightsaber drawn in gold-sparkler trails over a harbour. The caption read: 'Same energy. Different galaxy. Happy Fourth.' It has been pinned to the noticeboard beside Orvell's original postcard. The juxtaposition is pleasing.

stories.headline: Household Convenes in the Absence of Senior Staff; Proceedings Described as 'Largely Orderly' by One of the Three Parties Present
body: With Christine and Steve at elevation for the day, the three familiars were left to self-govern. By eleven o'clock, a loose coalition had formed, collapsed, and reformed around the question of who had last seen the small soft object that lives under the left cushion. Lucy declined to comment. Ellie had not known the object existed until Lucy declined to comment about it, and then could think of nothing else.

Jersey spent the morning on the warm patch of floor that receives direct sun until noon, then migrated to the sofa with the precision of a man who has charted this route for years and sees no reason to renegotiate. He was asleep when the coalition reached its second impasse, and remained asleep through the resolution, and was the only one of the three who appeared entirely satisfied with the day by the time the Jeep was heard in the drive.

familiars_corner: Lucy: Was at all times in the kitchen, as anyone could see, which is a complete alibi and she would like that noted formally.
Ellie: Spent twenty minutes introducing herself to the brass duck on the shelf, found it unresponsive, and has decided it is either very shy or very wise.
Jersey: The warm patch was excellent today. Nine out of ten. Would recommend.
founding_editor_note: That ridgeline story is good work — but I'd have checked the eastern team first. I built a deck off a disputed survey once. Took it down myself when I found out. Took it down level. — D.

in_brief.sport: Ridgeline elevation confirmed at 1,847 feet; Christine reached it without consulting the altitude chart, which the chart considers slightly rude but accurate.
in_brief.forecast: Clear through evening on the high ground; fog returns to the hollow by morning, right on schedule, same as it has done since 1962.

storyline_updates.title: The Third Fold
storyline_updates.summary: Resolved Saturday morning: the ridgeline was reached, the atmospheric inversion confirmed, and the 1962 survey dispute settled by presence — the eastern team was closer, as Christine's pencil margin note argued. Orvell was already there with a second brass duck and a flask of tea. The trail is now theirs. Arc closed with full honours. Two brass ducks sit on the shelf.

storyline_updates.title: The Northern Descent
storyline_updates.summary: New arc opening quietly: at the ridgeline, Steve was already looking at the northern descent before they left. It is not on any current trail map — Orvell said only 'it goes somewhere' before departing. The enchanted survey charts list the northern face as 'Unverified (Seasonal),' which is different from 'Disputed (Atmospheric)' in ways that neither Christine nor Steve has yet worked out. Day 2 should cover the first look at what 'Unverified (Seasonal)' actually means, and whether the brass duck pair on the shelf has any bearing on it.
